title: Crepidium ovalisepalum

The classification of Crepidium ovalisepalum is further categorized under the family Orchidaceae and the order Asparagales. The subfamily is Pleurothallidoideae, which is a subfamily under the family Orchidaceae. intro: Crepidium ovalisepalum, also known as the oval-sepal orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium ovalisepalum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects.
